title: Bun Bo Hue Umami Vietnamese Pork
description: A Vietnamese breakfast dish featuring pork, Thai chilli jam, and fermented shrimp paste for a bold umami flavor. This recipe serves 4-6 people and can be made in under an hour.
instructions: In a blender or food processor, combine Thai chilli jam, fish sauce, and fermented shrimp paste to make the umami sauce.
Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large pot over medium-high heat.
Add the ground pork and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until it is no longer pink, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the sliced onion and minced garlic to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the sliced lemongrass and grated ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the pork shoulder and umami sauce to the pot, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to medium-low and cook, covered, until the pork is tender, about 20-25 minutes.
Soak the rice noodles in hot water according to the package instructions, then drain and set aside.
Add the bean sprouts to the pot and cook, stirring constantly, until they are slightly tender, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the cooked noodles to the pot and stir to combine with the pork and sauce.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the bun bo hue in individual bowls with lime wedges and additional umami sauce on the side, if desired.
